name: Peanut Caramel Protein Twix Bars
description: A protein Twix: baked shortbread-style base, a real brown-sugar peanut caramel, and a thin chocolate shell. Adapted from a PB-powder-based protein twix — rebuilt without peanut-butter powder (not on hand) and leaned caramel-forward (caramel > PB > chocolate) for better macros. Subs: unflavored plant protein -> plain whey isolate; vanilla whey-casein -> vanilla whey; the PB-powder 'caramel' -> an actual brown-sugar + Fairlife caramel with a modest amount of real creamy PB; SF chopped chocolate -> semi-sweet chips (no SF chocolate in the library), trimmed thin. House calibration: real brown sugar in the caramel (molasses matters); Splenda for added sweetness elsewhere.

steps.instructionText: Base: whisk together the oat flour, plain whey isolate, vanilla whey, coconut flour, and Splenda.
Stir in the almond milk to form a stiff, pressable dough. Press firmly and evenly into a parchment-lined loaf pan or small square pan.
Bake at 325 F (160 C) for 10-12 minutes, until just set and dry to the touch. Cool completely before adding the caramel.
Peanut caramel: in a small saucepan over low heat, stir the brown sugar, Fairlife milk, and salt until dissolved and slightly thickened, 3-4 minutes.
Off the heat, stir in the peanut butter until smooth, then the vanilla whey. Spread over the cooled base and chill until set, ~30 minutes.
Chocolate shell: melt the chocolate chips with the coconut oil (short microwave bursts, stirring) and spread in a thin even layer over the caramel.
Chill until the chocolate is set, then lift out and slice into 8 bars. Cut cold for clean edges.

notes.text: Don't overbake the base — pull it as soon as it's set and dry on top. Overbaked, it turns rock-hard and the bars shatter instead of giving a clean twix-style snap.
Stir the vanilla whey into the caramel OFF the heat — protein powder added to a hot, actively-boiling caramel clumps and turns grainy. Take the pan off, let it stop bubbling, then stir it in.
The peanut butter is the main fat in the whole bar (everything else is lean). Don't add more than the 22 g — extra PB both blows the fat budget and softens the caramel so it won't set firm.
Chill the caramel layer firm BEFORE pouring the chocolate, or the warm chocolate melts into it and you lose the distinct twix layers.
Caramel-forward by design (caramel > PB > chocolate). To push it even more caramel-heavy, bump the brown sugar — it's fat-free, so it adds caramel flavor and carbs without touching the fat budget.
